introduction: A localized collection of pus in the peri-apical region of a tooth contained within the alveolar bone.

features: Painful swelling at the root of tooth
Tooth is tender to biting or percussion

non_drug: Drain abscess using local anaesthesia by root canal extirpation if tooth is to be retained
drug: Amoxicillin - Adult: 250 mg orally every 8 hours for 5 to 7 days
Amoxicillin - Child up to 10 years: 125 mg every 8 hours for 5-7 days, doubled in severe infections
Metronidazole - Adult: 200 mg orally every 8 hours for 3 - 7 days
Metronidazole - 1 - 3 years: 50 mg orally 8 hourly for 3 - 7 days
Metronidazole - 7-10 years: 100 mg 8 hourly for 3-7 days
Clindamycin - Adult: 150 - 300 mg every 6 hours; up to 450 mg every 6 hours in severe infections
Clindamycin - Child: 1 month – 18 years 3 - 6 mg/kg (max. 450 mg) every 6 hours
Clindamycin - Neonate Under 14 days 3-6 mg/kg 3 times daily
Clindamycin - Neonate 14-28 days 3-6 mg/kg (max. 450 mg) 4 times daily

Clindamycin - By deep intramuscular injection or by intravenous infusion, 0.6-2.7 g daily (in 2-4 divided doses) life threatening infection, up to 4.8 g daily; single doses above 600 mg by intravenous infusion only; single doses by intravenous infusion not to exceed 1.2 g
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Discontinue immediately if diarrhoea or colitis develops; monitor liver and renal function if treatment exceeds 10 days, and in neonates and infants; avoid rapid intravenous administration; avoid in acute porphyria
